title: luzula spadicea subsp. candollei
intro: Luzula spadicea subsp. candollei, also known as Candolle's woodrush, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Juncaceae. It is a perennial plant with grass-like leaves, native to the mountainous regions of Europe. Luzula spadicea subsp. candollei has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat wounds and skin irritations. The plant's roots can be used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which may help to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation, providing relief from symptoms of arthritis and other inflammatory conditions.
